scenario_config.name: ingest_isolation
_dataset_notes: Minimal dataset — purpose is to stress the ingest endpoint, not analytics.
2 hot weeks provide enough ingest target variety without heavy analytics query cost.
2 cold compacted weeks are present so the pre/post benchmark baseline is comparable to other scenarios.
_traffic_notes: Only the ingest scenario runs — all other traffic classes are set to rate 0 and are excluded.
ramp_mode: true ramps ingest from 0 to 60 req/sec linearly over 4 minutes.
The ramp reveals the ingest break-point: watch for where dropped_iterations first appear and where CPU pegs.
The break-point RPS is the server's sustainable ingest ceiling on this hardware.
VUs are sized generously (pre=50, max=200) so that drops come from server slowness, not VU starvation.
Run this BEFORE national_medium or national_high — it tells you whether 35–52 ingest RPS is even reachable.
If the ceiling is below 35 RPS, the server needs optimization or more workers before national scenarios are meaningful.

load_health.note: Marginal load: 1.2% of planned iterations were dropped. Latency numbers may be slightly optimistic because slow requests that never started are not counted. Consider reducing rates for a cleaner baseline.
